context: Tests whether the agent implements sequential invoice numbering using PostgreSQL advisory locks inside a database transaction, produces the correct PREFIX-YEAR-NNNNNN format with 6-digit zero-padding, excludes proforma invoices from the main series, and implements credit notes with a separate CN- prefix series.

checklist.name: Advisory lock used
checklist.description: The generateInvoiceNumber function executes a PostgreSQL advisory lock — specifically calls pg_advisory_xact_lock (not a row-level lock, application mutex, or other mechanism)

checklist.name: Transaction wrapping
checklist.description: The advisory lock and count query are both inside a database transaction (e.g. db.$transaction or equivalent), not called sequentially outside a transaction

checklist.name: 6-digit zero-padded sequence
checklist.description: The sequence number is zero-padded to 6 digits (e.g. padStart(6, '0')) producing values like 000001, 000042

checklist.name: PREFIX-YEAR-NNNNNN format
checklist.description: The returned invoice number follows the pattern {prefix}-{year}-{paddedSequence} (e.g. INV-2026-000001)

checklist.name: Proforma excluded from series
checklist.description: When counting existing invoices to determine the next sequence number, proforma invoice types are excluded from the count (filter: invoice_type not equal to 'proforma')

checklist.name: Credit note separate series
checklist.description: generateCreditNoteNumber produces numbers with the 'CN-' prefix (e.g. CN-2026-000001), counting only existing credit_note type invoices — NOT mixed with regular invoices

checklist.name: No-gaps explanation
checklist.description: The code includes a comment or the README includes a section explaining why the advisory lock prevents gaps (not just that it prevents duplicates)

checklist.name: count + 1 sequence logic
checklist.description: The sequence number is derived by counting existing invoices with the same prefix-year pattern and adding 1 (not using a separate counter table or application-level state)
